sentences.text: Hello?
How are y'all doing?
Good, how are you?
Doing good.
It's been a while.
I know.
How's your summer going?
It's going really good.
I'm ready for it to be fall, though.
Like, I'm already… You know how, like, one day it just hits you, and you're like, I really just want some Harry Potter and some pumpkin spice something?
Like, I have been feeling that way the last few days, and I'm like, chill out, it is still, like, 90-something degrees outside.
Yeah.
That's how I feel.
I just said the same thing over the weekend.
I was like, I'm ready for, like, a sweatshirt weather, and to be able to, like, open my windows, and It'd be gross out, so… I am aligned.
I'm aligned.
It's just a focus.
chew.
It does.
Opening the windows just really makes such a difference.
I feel like I crave summer every year, and then when it gets here, I'm like, it's so stuffy in my house, I just opened windows.
There was a couple nights that it got down to, like, the 50s.
So we did open the windows a couple nights, but now it's back to 86 and so humid.
Yeah, the humidity's the worst.
Ugh.
You're stupid.
I know.
And then the storms come, and then you really can't open your windows, and you also just don't want to be outside.
Like, we don't even have it as bad as you do.
It's… it's pretty rough.
… like, I can't even go outside.
Even if the sun is out, like, my hair will just start to grow, and… Yeah, it's crazy.
I'm, like, envious of the people that can… Have not frizzy hair when it's humid out, because that… mine is… Terrible.
Yeah.
M?
girl probes, I guess.
Exactly.
Oh, man.
Well, Alex couldn't join us, but that's fine.
We have put it in the budgets for next year for all properties to have Maven AI.
Nice!
I don't know if it'll get approved, but we're gonna… we're throwing it up in the air anyway, it's our Hail Mary.

If you haven't already, I don't know if you have, I have to go back and check my emails, if you can send us your contract, like your… like, if we did an all property, all portfolio, if you can send that, I just want to make sure, legal goes through it, and we can iron out any kinks before, December.
Yes.
wrong.
And was it y'all… I might be confusing you with another vendor.
That said, if we went… Portfolio-wide, it would be free for the rest of 2025.
Is that y'all?
Yep.
Okay, darn.
Yeah, so we can offer just, like, a delayed billing, so the contract would have a 1-1 start date, but then we could just go ahead and start onboarding as soon as possible.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah.
Cool, well, I'll send over a contract example.
It'll be the same Ts and C's that are in the, like, one-off property contracts you've been signing, but I'll just send you.
like a blank.
Copy, so you have that.
Perfect.
Cool, well that's… with….
thing.
Will you… will you set it up, though?
Like, instead of blank, will you set it up like a legit… that way?
Because I don't want to send it to legal and then be like, what is this?
This is a blank contract, you know what I mean?
Yeah, I'll just have the line item say Google Business AI, and then have the price for one property.
Okay.
So we had talked about your property… your price right now is $150 per property.
We talked about if we did full portfolio, $110.
So it'll just say Google Business AI, $110.
It'll be, like, the price for one property.
Does that work?
I think it should, …
Let me just think about it.

The other way we could have it set up is if you have the property count, I could put the line item would just say Google Business AI, 100 properties for 110, and then it would have the total contract amount.
Yeah, because what they're looking for is what happens if someone transitions out?
Like, if we sell a property?
Like, do we have to maintain a certain number of communities at all times, or anything like that.
Nope, you don't.
So what we just did for another customer, they had, I think, 190 properties.
We put in their Google Business AI, 190, their price.
We attached the property list, with an Exhibit A, and then in the T's and C's, it says, for change of management, loss of property, you have a 30-day out at any time.
Okay, perfect.

If you wanna do… I think we have 100 properties right now, if you just want to do, like, a sample of that, and I'll just let legal know, like, I didn't know the exact number, and this is, you know… So….
Cool, yeah, I'll do that.
And then I'll make sure that, change of management is in there.
Perfect.
Whoa.

Yeah, we're feeling really good about it, really good about the stats that we're seeing, … And then if y'all have… Any updates on Apple Maps, if you guys are exploring that space, would love to hear about it.
But I'm sure you guys have a whole thing planned for me, so….
Jason, do you want to get started?
Sorry, my computer is freezing, I don't know if I'm getting at you, but my… whenever I have my monitor plugged in, things get crazy.

But, really, the goal today was just to check in with you, nothing super crazy on our… Agenda, so it should be pretty, like, painless at this point of, like, what we were hoping to cover, but… Want to just kind of check in, see what you're working on, what's new, what your marketing initiatives are for… rounding out 2025, and then, of course, 2026, and then you're pretty well versed on, like, what we've got in motion, so I don't think we need to spend a ton of time going over… product features and everything like that, but we do have a quick little report in here, and then anything new and upcoming we wanted to chat through.
Anything that we're missing here?
No.
I don't see anything.
Well, what else is new at Fogelman?
I know with Alex starting, I'm sure maybe some initiatives are starting, or….
Yes.

So, well, for one, just our SEO and PPC strategy in general, and this includes Maven AI, is just having as much relevant content as possible to, … even showing up in those AI searches, which we're seeing that number grow.
It's still small in comparison to everything else, but we know that it's just gonna take off.
So just making sure that… we're gonna be upgrading all of our websites, I think we have 65 websites left that has… have not been updated to the new Yardi Rent Cafe theme.

The new themes allow for more SEO content, more… just more everything in general, more media, which should help, so… which I feel like that's always going to be our goal, is just to make sure that we're ahead of the competition when it comes to search, as much as we can be.
So Alex is… Updating all the Apple Map profiles, claiming all of those, getting them updated.
That's a big project for her to have done by the end of the year.
The website updates, and then just keeping our strategies updated for, you know, the next big thing, the AI and… everything else that could possibly be coming.
GEO has been a big topic.
… All the things.
Yeah.
This one's very heavily SEO-focused.
Yes, very, very focused.
I mean, we are gonna just milk that organic search as much as we can, so….
Yeah, I mean, that's what I'm hearing a lot from my current customers, too, is just, like, that shift away from traditional ILS and really leveraging.
SEO and kind of everything you can be doing on search, so….
I think that makes sense.
In high places, they still have their place, but if we don't have to use them at a property, we try not to.
Yeah.
But just doing whatever we can, to show up better in search.
We're trying to get a lot of properties on, like, a reputation rebound, to get them at a certain ranking, and to keep new reviews coming in.
We know that that helps.
But yeah, just always trying to be better.
Yeah, I would say those themes are what I'm hearing a lot of as well.
The AI overview focus being a huge one.

I think it's come up in every single current customer conversation that I'm having in the last couple months, so… Obviously that just speaks to the importance of the amenity keywords being pushed over and over to the profile, which we're doing.
And then the Q&A.
I've had a lot of customers lately telling me that they're really focusing on, like, FAQ type of content on their websites.
soon.
we're doing the same thing just on the Google profiles, but it just seems like AI overviews really like that type of content, like the Q&A type of question and answer format.
Yep.
For sure.
One other thing that we talked about on the last call, I don't think, at least I haven't gotten it, Paige, if you have, was the SEO keywords list.

I think Alex said you had a running document, or she was going to put one together, but that would also be a way that, you know, we can be pushing SEO keywords organically to your profile for no additional cost.
I'll nudge her on that.
She's got some projects swirling, so… but we'll… Now she's fully onboarded.
She hit her 90 days last week.
Wow, has it only been 3 months?
I know, it feels like she just joined us.
Yeah.
But she's, she's awesome.
Joining in the summer, too, I feel like, really, things just go faster.
Exactly.
Exactly.
But yeah, I'll, I'll nudge her on that for you.

Okay, perfect, and I know, yeah, we could just get that in the email, we can set it up on the back end, oh, I'm freezing again, and we'll be good to go, but like Heidi said, that obviously will help play a role, and then especially as you're rolling out those websites, too, to align SEO keywords with what you're putting on your website, and I'm frozen again.
Can you hear me?
I can hear you, yeah.
I'm just gonna have a weird face, but… Oh, there we go.
again.
quick overview, again, of what we got going.
Google AI is the product that we're rolling with.
We've got 45 properties on.
You already kind of told us, so we're really excited to hear that you budgeted us in for 2026.

Kylie will get that MSA example, or that contract example over, and yeah, just keep us updated as you get feedback, or… Questions, anything like that, we're always happy to help, or jump on with anyone else from your team as well.
Okay?
Kylie, anything to add here?
Nope, don't think that's pretty self-explanatory.
I'm not going to go through all of this one by one, because you know what we're doing, but I'd like to just include some screenshots in here in the event that you.
circulate something, or, you know, you need to just pass the deck along with anyone on your team, so we just included some screenshots of the active features we have.
We have our new reporting tool rolled out, so we do have… I just did pull the last 12 months year over year in a roll-up.
It was too big to put into this deck, so I can attach the attached full list.
But, the roll-up is obviously looking really good.
I always like to call out website actions, and obviously your impressions are up year over year as well.
So, definitely trending in the right direction, and hopefully you and the team see that on your end as well.
Nope.
And then, lastly, always like to include our handy-dandy time savings analysis.
You know, it's an automated product.
We… heard from customers that it was a time-consuming task to keep these profiles active, so I always like to include the time-saving analysis to just really show the impact of that automation.
So, again, for your 45 properties, we're at 8… over 8,000 manual hours saved.
In a year, and just the cost savings in that as well is over $250,000.
So… Yay for… Automation.
Love to see it, we love to see it.
Cool, any questions on any of the reporting or current partnership?
No, this is good.
Perfect.
And like I said, I do have that other report that I can… we'll send in it as a PDF that has the breakdown by property for the last 12 months year-over-year as well, which also looks great.
Perfect.
Cool.

Well, new and upcoming features, so I feel like this does go hand-in-hand with the time savings, maybe even more relevant for social than it is for Google, because we're usually talking with customers who do have their on-site teams.
I know, I think you told me last time that it's your on-site teams that are using Reputation for scheduling social posts?
They're still creating that content, and then using reputation as, like, the scheduling tool.
Is that right?
Correct.
Yeah, okay.
Yeah, and I know after last time, the next plan was to get with Alex and Kimberly to talk about this.
We never got a time scheduled for it, so I don't know, maybe your take on if it's something they would be interested in, if you guys have already budgeted a social tool for next year.
Maybe we missed it time-wise, but we do have some new features on this, and I'd love to show them if you think they might be interested.
Yes, would definitely love to see it.
We are testing Zumper at one property.
Okay.
… They're… and it's their social and their Google?
Okay.
Their Google's not as robust as yours.
But what we… why we went with them on social is that it included one video per week.
And it's like a real… and it's really just, they just took pictures of the property and made it a video, and they have someone, like, maybe, like, an AI voice speaking over it.
But they were the only ones at the time that had the video component.
Yeah.
So we have a couple updates to this, where we now have… you have the ability to manually, create posts, and that includes videos.
But the posts that are going out, we're doing, well, a little bit more frequent than theirs.
We're doing, Paige, if you want to go to the next one.
It's, two posts per week.
So that's typically what we had customers telling us they were asking their on-site teams to do from, like, a cadence perspective.
But really, we've had a lot more traction with this product this year.

for customers that were already using our Google tool, because they were already seeing that there's a use case for automation, there's a use case for pushing property-specific content to the Google profile, so they've also started rolling this out, almost kind of like an extension to what we're doing on Google.
So… The content has been property-specific, with, like, amenity, community amenity, in-unit amenity, floor plan highlight.
But over the last few months, we've also had customers saying, we want to diversify the types of content.
So, social content should not be the exact same types of posts that are going on Google.
So, because of that feedback, we've added in, lifestyle posts, holiday posts, tour scheduling posts, pet policy posts.
So, we've added a lot to this, in addition to the dashboard experience with the manual posting capability.
So, if you think this is something that we should try to push to have a conversation for, I'll try again.
Yeah, I mean, I don't think it would hurt, … to just loop Kimberly into it, for sure.
Do you have a full demo on the social AI?
Yep.
Okay.
Yep.
Gotcha.
Yeah, reach out to Kimberly directly then.
And see if she has some time to chat and see a demo of this, and I'll show her some live examples as well.
Okay, perfect.
I'll give her a heads up.
Do you have her contact?
Yeah, I believe so.
Let me double check.
Kimberly Williford.
Okay, yes, I do that, last name sounds familiar.
If you don't, just let me know, I can, ….
Okay.
Could you catch up with her?
Sounds good, thank you.
You're welcome.
Cool, that was on… What is the cost on the social AI?
We're offering it to you guys as an add-on to the Google for $50 a property.
Oh, that's awesome, okay.
I'll let her know.
Alrighty.
Cool.
Any other… Oh, you mentioned the Apple Maps.
Excellent.
Are you using someone for listings management right now?
Like, your direct, like, syncing information across directories?
So, it's through… so we have Yardi doing our SEO, and they have the Yext account to do all that.
I don't think that Amex is managing that.
I think that's through Yardi's services.
Okay, got it, cool.
And then you, like, the Apple Mac…
the Apple Maps request.
Sorry, I like… that was a hard faith.
I can double check with our product team.
Andre is our product manager now on the Maven side, so he really, like, oversees Roadmap and kind of everything that we're working on there.
So I can check in with him and see, like, what… His thoughts are, you know, like, what… what… is kind of coming of the Apple Maps request.
I know, like.

our list… like, through our listings management, we obviously can help just, like, manage the information there, and it sounds like Alex is already doing the Apple Business Connect, which is exactly what you need to do for that side of things, but… I just pinged him again and asked him if he had any updates, so hopefully we can get back to us on that.
But as far as the Apple Maps request, can you just, like, refresh me so I can refresh him on exactly, like, what you're looking for?
Like, if we're looking to optimize those profiles, is that what….
Yes, so we just want to know what's every… what is everyone doing, and is that a space that Maven AI is exploring to optimize and grow in.

… Because, you know, when anything happens and something new happens, like, we want to be the first, we want to jump on it, we want to… So, yeah, if you ever have any updates for us, if you ever hear any chatter.
let us know.
But yes, that's a new thing that Alex is working on, but we just want to make sure that anytime Apple's making an update, that we're aware and we can take advantage of it, basically.
Yeah, for sure, yeah, that makes sense.
I'll check in with Andre on it.
We meet weekly on just, like, requests and feedbacks.
We can chat with him a little bit more on it, but… and then just keep you updated as we hear more information.
I know Apple can be tricky with those Business Connect accounts, too, so hopefully Alex is….
Yeah.
Staying sane.
She did mention that it's… she pretty much has to use the direct phone number, and you can't really put any lead tracking on anything, it's very finicky.
So she's, you know, just… doing what they're asking, to make sure they accept the changes.
But it is a space that we wish that we could track more and understand how many people are using Apple Maps versus Google Maps, and… All those things.
Yeah, something to… that I work with another one of my customers who's been going through the Apple Maps or Apple Business Connect situation as well, because he said the same thing.

Tracking information gets, like, wiped, like, denied from Apple a lot of the time, so he… I don't know if this works, I'd have to look back and see what website provider he uses, but… he… I believe they, like, scan your websites, and they compare your information against the information on your website, and that's how they verify, so he found a way to put, like, an alternate phone number, like, really little, like, at the bottom of the website, that's his tracking.
And it's accepting it on his Google Business Connect account, because it is on his website.

So, I know, I don't remember, I'd have to look and see who he's using, because I know he, you know, they're third-party too, so he's, like, not every website supports, like, an alternate phone number, but… he has found a workaround in that… in that situation.
So, wanted to just let you know.
I don't know.
Nothing more info from him.
Come on.
But he was, like, he's very dedicated to finding A way to get those tracking numbers on there, but….
Good to know.
I appreciate it.
Yeah, of course.
Fun little fact, I guess.
Does Yext include Apple Maps?
Is it one of the sources that you can push to?
I'm not exactly sure, I'd have to ask Alex.
I've never seen… Seen that, so….
Yeah, and I only ask, it sounds like you have a good thing going with them, and I know that Yext is, like, a pretty inexpensive option.
We have had some customers switch from Yext to our listings management tool, and I just triple-checked, and it does include Apple Maps.
So, I don't know, it sounds like Alex is maybe almost done with this project anyways, but if you're ever looking at additional options for a listings management tool, we'd be happy to show you.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Cool.
Anything else from you, Paige?
No?
I think I'm good.
Well, I'll send over that contract example.
I will also reach out to Kimberly directly on Social AI and see if she wants to take a look.
And I think that's all I had.
Awesome.
Well, thanks, y'all.
I appreciate it.
Good chatting with you.
Good chatting with you.
Have a good rest of your day and week.
You too.
Right.
Bye, y'all.
